description:
For each of src/python/{cargo,buck,buildsystem,cfg} and src/examples/python-hello{,-deps}, add a pyproject.toml that:

- Declares [project] name = 'turnkey-<dir>' (e.g., turnkey-cargo).
- Declares [build-system] requires = ['hatchling'], build-backend = 'hatchling.build'.
- Remaps source files into turnkey/<name>/ via hatchling force-include:
    [tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel.force-include]
    '.' = 'turnkey/<name>'
- Lists external deps that this member actually uses (six probably belongs to whichever member imports it).
- Lists cross-member deps via dependencies = ['turnkey-cfg'] etc., wired with [tool.uv.sources] { workspace = true }.

Cross-member dep map (mirroring rules.star):
- turnkey-buck   → turnkey-buildsystem, turnkey-cargo, turnkey-cfg
- turnkey-cargo  → turnkey-cfg

No imports renamed yet, no workspace wired yet. After this ticket the new pyprojects exist but uv doesn't know about them.

Spot-check: 'uv pip install -e src/python/cfg/' in a throwaway venv and confirm 'from turnkey.cfg import ...' resolves. If hatchling force-include misbehaves in editable mode, fall back to hatch sources+packages or an editables shim before declaring this done.
title: Add per-member pyproject.toml with hatchling remap
